footer.tagline: Building a safe home for women and their children.

hero.title: Ametsgoien
hero.subtitle: Welcome, dignity and accompaniment
hero.description: Building a safe home for women and their children.

about.title: About AMETSGOIEN
about.description1: AMETSGOIEN works to provide comprehensive support to women and their children, prioritizing dignity, emotional security and compassionate care.
about.description2: We believe in the importance of building a safe, warm and humane home, where every woman can rediscover her strength, regain hope and move towards a stable life.

mariana.quote: Finding a safe and humane space makes the difference between fear and dignity. Ametsgoien represents that real welcome that many women need.
mariana.name: Mariana
mariana.fullStory:
Mariana arrived in the Basque Country more than a decade ago hoping to find a medical solution and be reunited with her son. As a single mother and migrant woman, she faced numerous difficulties from the start: lack of registration, absence of documentation, limited access to employment and, especially, the impossibility of finding decent housing for herself and her son.

For years she experienced discrimination in access to housing, both for having a child and for her origin. She shared spaces where she did not feel safe or welcome, and where even everyday actions were grounds for judgment. Added to these difficulties were obstacles to accessing public education and school scholarships, which made it even more complex to ensure her son's stability.

Despite everything, Mariana also found support networks in associations, collectives of migrant women and community spaces where she felt accompanied and understood. From her experience, she defends the importance of real welcome, based on willingness and empathy, free from discrimination.

Mariana highlights the value of initiatives like Ametsgoien, which offer close, warm and safe spaces, capable of accompanying women and families in situations of great vulnerability in a humane way. For her, welcoming is not just opening a house, but opening arms and bringing hearts closer.

emadolis.quote: As a mother, the most important thing is to feel protected and accompanied. A safe place can change everything.
emadolis.name: Emadolis
emadolis.fullStory:
Emadolis emigrated from Nicaragua seeking a better future and new opportunities. She arrived pregnant and, after being abandoned by the father of her child, found herself in a situation of extreme vulnerability. Without documentation, without work and with a newborn baby, she came to live on the street and face a particularly difficult birth alone.

The search for housing was one of the greatest difficulties. In many places she was denied rental for having a child, and in others conditions were imposed that even limited her baby's right to play or make noise. She experienced moments of great anguish, uncertainty and fear, especially not knowing where to sleep or how to feed her child.

In the midst of that situation, Emadolis found human and emotional support in people who offered her listening, words of encouragement and accompaniment. That support was key to regaining strength and moving forward.

From her experience as a mother, she defends the need for safe spaces where women and children can live together without fear, with empathy and respect. She considers it essential to have associations like Ametsgoien, which offer protection, closeness and the possibility of feeling, even for a time, like home.

berita.quote: Without support and without housing, life becomes unbearable. Associations that truly accompany change destinies.
berita.name: Berita
berita.fullStory:
Berita decided to emigrate to offer a better future to her children and the possibility of accessing studies that were inaccessible in her country. She arrived in the Basque Country with expectations of work and stability, but soon encountered a very different reality. The person who promised her support upon arrival did not fulfill what was agreed, which led her to be left alone and without a place to live.

During her first months she went through extreme situations, including a night sleeping in a park. Thanks to timely help from supportive people, she managed to find temporary accommodation and, after a constant search, accessed her first job as a live-in worker. That job was a very hard experience, marked by exhaustive hours, unequal treatment and a strong physical and emotional impact.

The lack of documentation and payroll prevented her for years from accessing her own housing, forcing her to accept precarious conditions in order to support herself and send money to her children. This situation even led her to delay family reunification for fear of not being able to offer them a safe environment.

Berita emphasizes the importance of having associations that accompany, guide and protect migrant women, especially those with young children. For her, initiatives like Ametsgoien are fundamental to prevent more women from going through situations of exclusion and to build a more just and humane welcome.

whoWeAre.title: Who are we?
whoWeAre.p1b: a married couple from Alonsotegi with three teenage children who decided to transform an intimate concern into a collective project.
whoWeAre.p2: It all began with a story that deeply marked them:
whoWeAre.p2quote: I couldn't understand how a woman with a newborn baby could be on the streets in Bilbao in 2023
whoWeAre.p2b: That case, which came through the CVX solidarity network and the Ignacio Ellacuría Center, made them ask: what can we do?
whoWeAre.p3quote: If institutions can't provide a solution, we decided to go into debt with love
whoWeAre.p3b: That's how Ametsgoien was born, a 12-room house in Orduña converted into a safe home for women with children in their care.
whoWeAre.historyP1: During the Ukrainian crisis, the family welcomed four women and a translator into their own home.
whoWeAre.historyP1quote: We went from five to nine in the house. We had to reorganize everything, but our children experienced it naturally
whoWeAre.historyP2: That experience confirmed they were ready for something bigger. With a family and bank loan, they acquired a large house for 185,000 euros. And what came next exceeded all expectations:
whoWeAre.historyP2c: from Barcelona, Burgos, San Sebastian, Balmaseda, Bilbao and Orduña came together to sand, paint, varnish and bring this collective dream to life.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.text: To provide safe welcome, comprehensive support and personalized accompaniment to women and their families, offering tools for personal development, job training and economic autonomy, always with respect for their dignity and human rights.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.text: To be a reference organization in the reception and accompaniment of women, recognized for its transformative impact on the lives of the people we serve, and for contributing to a more inclusive, just and supportive society.

housing.title: Safe Home
housing.text: A 12-room house in Orduña, renovated through the effort of dozens of volunteers, where women and their children find a dignified and welcoming space.

support.title: Comprehensive Support
support.text: Personalized assistance with administrative procedures, job search, training and everything necessary to achieve autonomy.

community.title: Community Solidarity
community.text: A project built "drop by drop" by a community of volunteers who believe in the transformative power of mutual aid.

cta.title: Join our cause
cta.subtitle: Your support can transform lives. Together we can build a better future for women and their families.

activities.title: Activities and Workshops
activities.subtitle: Spaces for training, personal growth and community gathering designed to support women on their journey toward autonomy and well-being.

faq.title: Questions about how to collaborate?
faq.subtitle: We answer the most frequently asked questions about how you can help us. If you have any other questions, don't hesitate to contact us.

q1.question: How can I collaborate with Ametsgoien?
q1.answer: There are several ways to collaborate with our association: you can make a one-time donation through this website by card, Bizum or bank transfer. You can also join as a volunteer to support the daily activities of the shelter. Another valuable way to help is to spread our work on social media and among your network to give visibility to our mission.

q2.question: How can I get tax deductions for my donations?
q2.answer: Ametsgoien is a recently established association. According to current tax regulations, non-profit organizations can issue tax deduction certificates after three years from their foundation. Once this period is completed, we will automatically send you the annual certificate to include in your tax return.

section1.title: 1. Data Controller
section1.intro: The data controller for personal data collected through this website is the AMETSGOIEN association, a non-profit organization dedicated to social and community support.
section1.name: Name:
section1.nameValue: AMETSGOIEN ASSOCIATION FOR WELCOMING AND SOCIOCULTURAL INTEGRATION
section1.contact: For any questions related to personal data protection, you can contact us through the means provided above.

section2.title: 2. Applicable Legal Framework
section2.text: The processing of personal data is carried out in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council (General Data Protection Regulation – GDPR), as well as Organic Law 3/2018 on the Protection of Personal Data and guarantee of digital rights (LOPDGDD).

section3.title: 3. Personal Data Processed
section3.intro: Through this website, the following personal data may be collected:
donationItems: Payment data processed by Stripe (bank card)

section4.title: 4. Purpose of Processing
section4.intro: The personal data provided will be processed exclusively for the following purposes:
purposes: To respond to inquiries or requests for information.
To manage requests for collaboration or contact with the entity.
To maintain communications related to AMETSGOIEN's activity, when the user has expressly authorized it.

section5.title: 5. Legal Basis for Processing
section5.text: The legal basis for data processing is the express consent of the user, given when submitting the contact form and accepting this privacy policy.

section6.title: 6. Data Retention
section6.text: Personal data will be retained only for the time necessary to address the inquiry made and, subsequently, during the legally required periods for compliance with possible legal obligations.

section7.title: 7. Recipients and Data Processors
section7.intro: Data will not be transferred to third parties, except for legal obligation. However, for the proper provision of the service, AMETSGOIEN uses the following external providers who act as data processors:
section7.stripe: (payment processing): Payment data is processed securely through Stripe, Inc., which complies with the PCI-DSS standard. AMETSGOIEN does not store complete bank card data. More information at
section7.vercel: (frontend hosting): For hosting the website at www.ametsgoien.org. More information at
section7.railway: (backend and database hosting): For hosting the REST API server and PostgreSQL database. More information at
section7.sendgrid: (email service): For sending donation confirmations and responses to contact forms. More information at
section7.guarantees: With all providers, the guarantees required by current regulations have been adopted through confidentiality agreements and contractual clauses.

section8.title: 8. Rights of Data Subjects
section8.intro: The user may exercise the following rights at any time:
section8.complaint: Likewise, you have the right to file a complaint with the Spanish Data Protection Agency (AEPD) through its website:

section9.title: 9. Security Measures
section9.text: AMETSGOIEN adopts the necessary technical and organizational measures to ensure an adequate level of security appropriate to the risk, preventing loss, alteration, unauthorized access or improper disclosure of personal data.

section10.title: 10. Refund and Cancellation Policy
section10.intro: Donations made to AMETSGOIEN are voluntary contributions intended to finance reception and social support projects.
section10.oneTimeText: Once the donation is processed, it is final. If you detect an error in your donation, contact us at ametsgoien@gmail.com within 48 hours and we will evaluate your case individually.
section10.recurringIntro: You can cancel your subscription at any time at no additional cost. To do so:
recurringSteps: Access the management link sent in the confirmation email
section10.recurringNote: The cancellation will be effective from the next billing period. Amounts already paid are non-refundable.

section11.title: 11. Policy Modifications
section11.text: AMETSGOIEN reserves the right to modify this privacy policy to adapt it to legislative changes or new data processing. Any modification will be published on this same page.
